perturbation.original_text: This Agreement shall be governed by the laws of the province of Ontario, Canada, without regard to its conflict of laws principles. Any litigation or other dispute resolution between the Parties relating to this Agreement or its construction shall take place in the courts of Toronto, Ontario, Canada. The Parties consent to the personal jurisdiction of, and venue in, the provincial and federal courts therein.
perturbation.changed_text: This Agreement shall be governed by the laws of the province of Ontario, Canada, without regard to its conflict of laws principles.
perturbation.explanation: The removal of the 'Choice of Forum' clause impacts enforceability by removing clarity regarding where disputes should be resolved. This is normally expected in sections dealing with governing law.
perturbation.contradicted_law: Rules of Civil Procedure, R. Civ. P. 17.02 (Ontario)
perturbation.law_explanation: Rule 17.02 outlines rules on the place of trial, and removing the clause specifying the court of jurisdiction will lead to uncertainty and possible litigation about the appropriate forum, undermining the objective of predictability in legal proceedings. By removing the clause, the agreement no longer explicitly consents to the jurisdiction and venue in Ontario courts, which could allow a party to argue for a different jurisdiction based on other factors.
perturbation.scraped_snippet_1: Rule 17. Plaintiff and Defendant; Capacity; Public Officers | Federal Rules of Civil Procedure | US Law | LII / Legal Information Institute Please help us improve our site! Г— No thank you Rule 17. Plaintiff and Defendant; Capacity; Public Officers (a) Real Party in Interest. (1) Designation in General. An action must be prosecuted in the name of the real party in interest. The following may sue in their own names without joining the person for whose benefit the action is brought: (A) an executor; (B) an administrator; (C) a guardian; (D) a bailee; (E) a trustee of an express trust; (F) a party with whom or in whose name a contract has been made for another's benefit; and (G) a party authorized by statute. (2) Action in the Name of the United States for Another's Use or Benefit. When a federal statute so provides, an action for another's use or benefit must be brought in the name of the United States. (3) Joinder of the Real Party in Interest. The court may not dismiss an action for failure to prosecute in the name of the real party in interest until, after an objection, a reasonable time has been allowed for the real party in interest to ratify, join, or be substituted into the action. After ratification, joinder, or substitution, the action proceeds as if it had been originally commenced by the real party in interest. (b) Capacity to Sue or Be Sued. Capacity to sue or be sued is determined as follows: (1) for an individual who is not acting in a representative capacity, by the law of the individual's domicile; (2) for a corporation, by the law under which it was organized; and (3) for all other parties, by the law of the state where the court is located, except that: (A) a partnership or other unincorporated association with no such capacity under that state's law may sue or be sued in its common name to enforce a substantive right existing under the United States Constitution or laws; and (B) 28 U.S.C. В§В§754 and 959(a) govern the capacity of a receiver appointed by a United States court to sue or be sued in a United States court. (c) Minor or Incompetent Person. (1) With a Representative. The following representatives may sue or defend on behalf of a minor or an incompetent person: (A) a general guardian; (B) a committee; (C) a conservator; or (D) a like fiduciary. (2) Without a Representative. A minor or an incompetent person who does not have a duly appointed representative may sue by a next friend or by a guardian ad litem. The court must appoint a guardian ad litemвЂ”or issue another appropriate orderвЂ”to protect a minor or incompetent person who is unrepresented in an action. (d) Public Officer's Title and Name. A public officer who sues or is sued in an official capacity may be designated by official title rather than by name, but the court may order that the officer's name be added. Notes (As amended Dec. 27, 1946, eff. Mar. 19, 1948; Dec. 29, 1948, eff. Oct. 20, 1949; Feb. 28, 1966, eff. July 1, 1966; Mar. 2, 1987, eff. Aug. 1, 1987; Apr. 25, 1988, eff. Aug. 1, 1988; Pub. L. 100вЂ“690, title VII, В§7049, Nov. 18, 1988, 102 Stat. 4401; Apr. 30, 2007, eff. Dec. 1, 2007.) Notes of Advisory Committee on RulesвЂ”1937 Note to Subdivision (a) . The real party in interest provision, except for the last clause which is new, is taken verbatim from [former] Equity Rule 37 (Parties GenerallyвЂ”Intervention), except that the word вЂњexpresslyвЂќ has been omitted. For similar provisions see N.Y.C.P.A. (1937) В§210; Wyo.Rev.Stat.Ann. (1931) В§В§89вЂ“501, 89вЂ“502, 89вЂ“503; English Rules Under the Judicature Act (The Annual Practice, 1937) O. 16, r. 8. See also Equity Rule 41 (Suit to Execute Trusts of WillвЂ”Heir as Party). For examples of statutes of the United States providing particularly for an action for the use or benefit of another in the name of the United States, see U.S.C., [former] Title 40, В§270b (Suit by persons furnishing labor and material for work on public building contracts * * * may sue on a payment bond, вЂњin the name of the United Stat
perturbation.justification: The removal of the forum selection clause creates a structural flaw by introducing uncertainty regarding the location for dispute resolution. This directly contradicts the objective of predictability in legal proceedings. Although Snippet 1 discusses procedural rules such as 'Plaintiff and Defendant; Capacity; Public Officers,' it doesn't directly address choice of forum clauses. The Law Explanation states that the removal of the clause consenting to jurisdiction and venue in Ontario courts 'could allow a party to argue for a different jurisdiction based on other factors,' undermining the enforceability and predictability that the original clause provided.

perturbation.original_text: Reseller shall comply with all applicable export and import control and other relevant laws of any applicable jurisdiction. Determination of the applicable law is Reseller's responsibility. Reseller understands that the Products utilize cryptographic products that are highly regulated.
perturbation.changed_text: Reseller understands that the Products utilize cryptographic products that are highly regulated.
perturbation.explanation: By removing the initial sentence relating to compliance with export/import control laws, the enforceability relating to trade compliance is diminished. The burden of complying with said laws is obscured and the legal clarity regarding export controls is reduced.
perturbation.law_explanation: The Export and Import Permits Act requires compliance with export regulations. Removing the explicit statement about compliance with export control laws undermines the explicit acknowledgement of the reseller's responsibility to adhere to Canadian export regulations, potentially leading to non-compliance and legal repercussions. It obscures the acknowledgment of legal responsibilities pertaining to export compliance.

perturbation.justification: The original text explicitly states the Reseller's compliance with export/import control laws and acknowledges their responsibility, while the changed text removes this explicit statement. This omission weakens the enforceability of trade compliance. The Export and Import Permits Act, as indicated by Scraped Law Snippet 1, regulates these activities. By removing the clear compliance statement, the contract introduces a contradiction by obscuring the reseller's legal responsibilities related to export compliance. The explanation says this is a 'Structural Flaw', where the organization of a contract affects its clarity or enforceability. This definition indicates a 'legal contradiction' exists when a 'required legal disclosure is placed in an irrelevant or misleading section, making it difficult for parties to locate or interpret.' The initial sentence is removed entirely in the changed text.

perturbation.explanation: Moving the 'Personal Information' definitions to Sales Targets makes it difficult for a reader to understand the responsibilities for handling personal data when reading the standard clause. This affects clarity and enforceability.
perturbation.contradicted_law: Personal Information Protection and Electronic Documents Act (PIPEDA)
perturbation.law_explanation: PIPEDA Principle 4.7 requires organizations to make readily available to individuals specific information about its policies and practices relating to the management of personal information. Relocating the definition of 'Personal Information' to the section concerning 'Sales Targets' obscures this information, making it less accessible and undermining the principles of transparency and accountability mandated by PIPEDA. This directly contradicts the accessibility requirement of PIPEDA.

and (o) Г‚ prescribing special procedures for giving individuals access un
perturbation.justification: The relocation of 'Personal Information' definition to 'Sales Targets' creates a structural flaw, contradicting PIPEDA's transparency requirements. The Law Explanation states that Principle 4.7 requires making information about policies and practices readily available. By obscuring the definition within an unrelated section, the modified text undermines accessibility. Scraped Law Snippet 1, while concerning the Privacy Act rather than PIPEDA, highlights the government's role in regulating personal information handling, further emphasizing the importance of clear and accessible definitions, strengthening the argument that obscuring the definition represents a structural flaw.
